attacking_style: Does not attack traditionally; dominates through sustained power in time trials (flat to moderately technical) and aggressive front-running in echelon/crosswind scenarios. Non-ITT aggression limited to opportunistic breakaway surges exploiting descent power.
team_tactics: Primary role as super-domestique: extended high-tempo pulls to create splits and selections (20km+ from finish), pacing leaders on climbs before dropping back, and leading out sprinters when available. Given freedom in ITTs and occasional breakaways.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling ITTs (especially sub-35km) where sustained diesel power dominates; crosswind exposed roads for echelon creation; technical descents where power trumps handling. Vulnerable on repeated climbs, summit finishes, and punchy short ITTs under 12km.
vulnerability: Misjudges sprint timing when racing for himself (launches too early despite superior power). Struggles in mixed-terrain long ITTs against climbing specialists. Form periodization shows weakness in short punchy TTs vs sustained efforts. Technical wet conditions expose handling limits.
key_patterns: Five consecutive Giro ITT wins; dominates Italian GT time trials regardless of length (8.6-34km).
Repeatedly nearly wins sprints as non-sprinter through raw power; loses by mistiming effort initiation.
Initiates critical echelon splits in crosswinds before key sectors, eliminating favorites through positioning attacks.
Exceptional recovery allows domestique work day-after ITT wins; paces climbs then chases on flats.
Loses 9-22 seconds in final kilometers of ITTs; negative splits favor technical riders over pure power.
Sacrifices GC ambitions for team leaders despite top-10 climbing capacity on isolated summit finishes.
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta showed peak ITT form (56kph avg, beating Evenepoel) plus newfound sprint competitiveness, suggesting evolving tactical versatility beyond pure time trialing.
